mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate profit visibility and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and inflation signals strong demand pressures that boost corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL shows no significant correlation with SPX returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer demand, reducing corporate revenue expectations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in nonfarm payrolls above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals stronger corporate output and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending and inflation signals strong demand pressures that boost corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS and CPIAUCSL shows no significant correlation with SPX returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.
